checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=30,911,221.42 (2009-12-31) -> IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=57,104,372.72 (2010-12-31), an 84.7% increase versus 5.1% revenue growth in the same period

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=48,946,718.07 (FY2009) -> NetIncomeLoss=187,894,712.78 (FY2013), a 283.8% increase, while NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=778,276,917.31 (FY2009) -> NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=966,435,544.91 (FY2013), only a 24.2% increase

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: Gross margin computed as (Revenues-CostOfServices)/Revenues: FY2009 = (3,861,732,886.76-1,313,492,231.60)/3,861,732,886.76 = 66.0%; FY2013 = (5,072,288,854.64-1,870,176,305.76)/5,072,288,854.64 = 63.1% - a gradual, non-abrupt decline consistent with modest cost inflation, not a sudden anomaly
evidence.location: Revenues and CostOfServices, FY2009 and FY2013

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=39,623,571.30 (2009-12-31) -> InventoryNet=50,805,974.31 (2013-12-31), tracking roughly in line with 31.3% revenue growth over the same period

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Quarterly NetIncomeLoss shows consistent seasonality mirroring Revenues seasonality: NetIncomeLoss(2011-07-01/2011-09-30)=44,663,238.23, NetIncomeLoss(2012-07-01/2012-09-30)=59,334,215.94, NetIncomeLoss(2013-07-01/2013-09-30)=56,154,595.42 align with Revenues(2011-07-01/2011-09-30)=1,241,126,628.55, Revenues(2012-07-01/2012-09-30)=1,243,161,301.23, Revenues(2013-07-01/2013-09-30)=1,291,554,904.39, all peaking in Q3 each year

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent/ReceivablesNetCurrent ratio: 2009-12-31 = 10,910,586.54/473,040,458.24=2.31%; 2011-12-31 = 11,448,688.32/596,292,632.34=1.92%; 2013-12-31 = 13,397,233.11/614,249,902.74=2.18% - a modest dip in 2011 followed by recovery, not a persistent decline

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2012-03-19 amending a previously filed 10-K, with restated/updated balance sheet and income statement data spanning multiple prior periods (2007-2011)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Financial series data provided quarterly and annually from 2007-12-31 through 2014-03-31 across Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, OperatingCashFlow, Receivables, Goodwill, Intangibles, Inventory, Liabilities and related concepts, spanning multiple 10-K and 10-Q filings

top_signals: 10-K/A amendment filed 2012-03-19 restating multiple prior-period balance sheet and income statement figures, coupled with an extended series of SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD filings in 2011-2013 suggesting a comment-letter review process

Goodwill and intangible assets grew far faster than revenue in FY2010 (Goodwill +64.4%, Intangibles +84.7% vs Revenue +5.1%), though this is plausibly acquisition-driven rather than manipulative capitalization
Net income grew ~284% from FY2009 to FY2013 while operating cash flow grew only ~24% over the same period, a notable long-run divergence between reported earnings growth and cash generation
